methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $5.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Graphyte, a Series A climate and energy company, carries a current post-money valuation of $200M following $57M in total capital raised. Valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of eight same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers—including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.—alongside one public comparable (ticker: LNZA).

Two valuation methods yield triangulated results. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 2.0x Series A-to-next-stage step-up multiplier to the $200M base, implies $400M. Peer-median valuation across the eight-company Climate & Energy cohort yields $305M, with a range spanning $110M (minimum) to $5.0B (maximum). The median peer valuation reflects meaningful dispersion, driven in part by outliers like Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B versus lower-stage or smaller-scale peers.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $259M (low) to $460M (high), with a median point estimate of $352M. This range sits modestly above Graphyte's current $200M mark, consistent with progress typically expected between Series A and Series B. Key uncertainties include technology maturation timelines, capital intensity trajectory post-Series A, and whether peer dispersion ($110M–$5.0B) reflects genuine valuation variance or sector-specific stage and technology heterogeneity. The limited public comparables in climate tech constrains cross-market benchmarking.
